item.title: “This city is built of bones, Plastered with flesh and blood; Within dwells aging and death, Conceit and hypocrisy.” “Even the king’s splendid chariot wears…
item.description:
“This city is built of bones,
Plastered with flesh and blood;
Within dwells aging and death,
Conceit and hypocrisy.”

“Even the king’s splendid chariot wears out,
So too this body eventually ages.
But the Dhamma of the good does not age —
So the good indeed speak to the good.”

“A person of little learning,
Like an ox, grows old;
His flesh increases,
But not his wisdom.”

From: Dhammapada 146-156
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: “Monks, I will teach you about things that are bound and about binding. Listen. And what, monks, are the things that are bound? What is…
item.description:
“Monks, I will teach you about things that are bound and about binding. Listen. And what, monks, are the things that are bound? What is binding? Form, monks, is a thing that is bound. Desire and lust for form is the binding of form. Feeling … Perception … Formations … Consciousness, monks, is a thing that is bound. Desire and lust for consciousness is the binding of consciousness. These, monks, are called the things that are bound. This is the binding.”

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 22.120
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: “And what, monks, is the practice of restraint? Here, monks, when a monk sees a form with the eye, he does not grasp the general…
item.description:
“And what, monks, is the practice of restraint? Here, monks, when a monk sees a form with the eye, he does not grasp the general feature, does not grasp the particular feature. Those causes whereby, if the eye faculty were not restrained, covetousness, sorrow, evil, unwholesome states would arise — he restrains that cause, guards the eye faculty, practices restraint of the eye. When he hears a sound … smells an odor … tastes a flavor … feels a touch … cognizes a phenomenon with the mind, he does not grasp the general feature, does not grasp the particular feature. Those causes whereby, if the mind faculty were not restrained, covetousness, sorrow, evil, unwholesome states would arise — he restrains that cause, guards the mind faculty, practices restraint of the mind. This, monks, is called the practice of restraint.”

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 4.164
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: “One who has cut off craving, Without remainder, Dried up the swift stream — That monk abandons the near and far shore, Like a serpent…
item.description:
“One who has cut off craving,
Without remainder,
Dried up the swift stream —
That monk abandons the near and far shore,
Like a serpent sheds its worn-out skin.”

“One who has destroyed conceit,
Without remainder,
As a great flood destroys a fragile reed —
That monk abandons the near and far shore,
Like a serpent sheds its worn-out skin.”

From: Sutta Nipāta 1.1
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - “Venerable Bharadvāja, what is the cause, what is the reason why these young monks, youthful, with black hair, endowed with the blessing of youth,…
item.description:
- “Venerable Bharadvāja, what is the cause, what is the reason why these young monks, youthful, with black hair, endowed with the blessing of youth, in the prime of life, do not indulge in sensual pleasures and live the perfectly pure holy life for their whole life, completing it fully?” - “Great king, the Blessed One, the Knower, the Seer, the Arahant, the Perfectly Enlightened One, has said: ‘Come, monks, regarding those who are like mothers, establish a mother’s mind. Regarding those who are like sisters, establish a sister’s mind. Regarding those who are like daughters, establish a daughter’s mind.’ This, great king, is the cause, this is the reason why these young monks … live the holy life completely.”

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 35.127
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: “Monks, for any monk or nun whose greed is not abandoned, whose hatred is not abandoned, whose delusion is not abandoned — that one is…
item.description:
“Monks, for any monk or nun whose greed is not abandoned, whose hatred is not abandoned, whose delusion is not abandoned — that one is called one who has not crossed the ocean with its waves, its whirlpools, its sharks, its demons.

But, monks, for any monk or nun whose greed is abandoned, whose hatred is abandoned, whose delusion is abandoned — that one is called one who has crossed the ocean with its waves, its whirlpools, its sharks, its demons, who has crossed to the far shore, a brahmin standing on dry land.

One who has abandoned greed, hatred, and delusion
Has crossed this ocean with its sharks, demons,
Its terrifying waves — so very hard to cross —
He has passed beyond all bonds.

He has abandoned death,
Without attachments,
He has abandoned suffering,
With no further becoming.
Having reached the goal, he is immeasurable —
I say such a one
Has blinded the King of Death.”

From: Itivuttaka 69
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Sivaka: Impermanent are these houses, Built again and again. Seeking the house-builder, Birth is suffering. O house-builder, you are seen! From now on, you…
item.description:
Elder Sivaka:

Impermanent are these houses,
Built again and again.
Seeking the house-builder,
Birth is suffering.

O house-builder, you are seen!
From now on, you cannot
Build a house again.
All your rafters are broken,
The ridge-pole is shattered.
The mind has been pushed from the stream,
Here, it is destroyed.

From: Theragāthā 2.32
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Four wheels, nine doors, Full of defilement, tied by greed — Mired in mud, O great hero, What will be the future state Of such…
item.description:
Four wheels, nine doors,
Full of defilement, tied by greed —
Mired in mud,
O great hero,
What will be the future state
Of such a human birth?

(The Blessed One):
Having cut off delight and bondage,
Sensual desire and evil,
Having destroyed the root of craving —
Such will be his future state.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 1.29
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Standing to one side, the deity Subrahmā recited this verse to the Blessed One: This mind is always afraid, This mind is always agitated, What…
item.description:
Standing to one side, the deity Subrahmā recited this verse to the Blessed One:
This mind is always afraid,
This mind is always agitated,
What is wished does not arise,
What is not wished does arise.
If there is a way to be without fear,
Please tell me what I ask.

(The Blessed One):
Apart from enlightenment and austerity,
Apart from sense restraint,
Apart from abandoning all —
I see these things
That lead to safety
For all beings.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 2.17
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: “What do you think, Māgandiya? Have you ever seen or heard of a king or a king’s minister who, while enjoying the five strands of…
item.description:
“What do you think, Māgandiya? Have you ever seen or heard of a king or a king’s minister who, while enjoying the five strands of sensual pleasure fully and completely, without abandoning craving for sensual pleasures, without removing the fever of sensual pleasures, has dwelt, dwells, or will dwell with craving cut off and mind inwardly peaceful?”
“No, Master Gotama.”

From: Majjhima Nikāya 75 – Māgandiyasutta
📖 Read this sutta
